title: [ipv6_addr.h:68-91] struct in6_addr fallback assumes uint8_t s6_addr only
description:
Completeness: Platform compatibility category.

The struct in6_addr fallback definition at lines 88-90:


This is the minimal definition. However, POSIX struct in6_addr and Zephyr's version include additional union members:
- s6_addr16[8] (uint16_t array)
- s6_addr32[4] (uint32_t array)
- s6_addr (the uint8_t array)

Code that uses .s6_addr (byte access) works with both. But if any code tried to use .s6_addr32 with the fallback definition, it would fail to compile.

The current LICHEN code only uses .s6_addr (e.g., ipv6_addr.c:137, 181, 241-249), so this is safe. But the API exports struct in6_addr, and external callers might expect the full union.

Consider adding the union members to the fallback for compatibility, or document that only .s6_addr is guaranteed.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation note that only s6_addr is guaranteed in fallback definition
